content:
A black hole is like a giant space vacuum cleaner, but way stronger and cooler! It starts when a big star runs out of energy and falls in on itself, squishing into a tiny spot that’s super heavy. This tiny spot has such strong gravity—that’s the force that pulls things together—that nothing can escape it, not even light! That’s why it looks black, because no light can get out for us to see.

Imagine if you tried to swim out of a whirlpool that pulls you down faster and faster. A black hole’s gravity is like that, but much stronger. Once something gets too close, it’s pulled in and can’t get back out. That’s why black holes are like invisible space traps! Even though we can’t see them, scientists know they’re there because they watch how stars and light behave nearby. Black holes are one of space’s coolest mysteries!
